sub_modules.title: Introduction to AIP
sub_modules.content: Pilots must gather information about ATC procedures, frequencies, navigation aids, weather services, and airspace restrictions before flight.

sub_modules.title: Primary vs Secondary Sources
sub_modules.content: Charts like Jeppesen are secondary sources, while AIP issued by national authorities is the primary and official source.

sub_modules.title: AIP Definition
sub_modules.content: AIP is an official publication containing essential and lasting aeronautical information issued by a state.

sub_modules.title: AIP Supplements
sub_modules.content: Temporary changes to AIP information published separately.

sub_modules.title: NOTAM Definition
sub_modules.content: Short-term or urgent information about changes in aeronautical facilities, services, or hazards.

sub_modules.title: AIP Structure
sub_modules.content: Divided into GEN (General), ENR (En Route), and AD (Aerodromes).

sub_modules.title: Location Indicators
sub_modules.content: Four-letter ICAO codes identifying airports and centers, where first two letters represent country.

sub_modules.title: NOTAM Types
sub_modules.content: NOTAMN (new), NOTAMR (replacement), NOTAMC (cancellation).

sub_modules.title: AFIS
sub_modules.content: Aerodrome Flight Information Service provides essential information for safe operations at aerodromes.

sub_modules.title: ATIS
sub_modules.content: Automatic broadcast of routine aerodrome information to reduce radio workload.

sub_modules.title: Meteorological Information
sub_modules.content: Includes METAR, TAF, SIGMET, and weather charts for flight safety.

sub_modules.title: VOLMET
sub_modules.content: Continuous broadcast of meteorological information via radio.

sub_modules.title: Navigation Aids
sub_modules.content: Includes VOR, DME, and NDB with frequencies, identifiers, and operational details.

sub_modules.title: Significant Points
sub_modules.content: Waypoints defined by coded names and coordinates, often based on VOR/DME fixes.

sub_modules.title: Navigation Warnings
sub_modules.content: Includes restricted, prohibited, danger areas, and military zones.

sub_modules.title: Aerodrome Categories
sub_modules.content: Public Use License or Ordinary License determining accessibility of aerodromes.

sub_modules.title: Aerodrome Communication
sub_modules.content: Includes tower, approach, ATIS, and AFIS frequencies and services.

sub_modules.title: Landing Aids
sub_modules.content: Includes ILS, glide path, localizer, and DME systems for approach and landing.

sub_modules.title: Search and Rescue
sub_modules.content: Procedures and services available in case of emergency situations.

chapter_name: Chapter 2 - Fuel Policy and Fuel Monitoring

sub_modules.title: Universal Fuel Policy
sub_modules.content: Fuel planning follows EASA Fuel Policy ensuring sufficient fuel for trip, reserves, and contingencies.

sub_modules.title: Trip Fuel
sub_modules.content: Fuel required for the planned flight including climb, cruise, descent, approach, and landing.

sub_modules.title: Realistic Planning
sub_modules.content: Trip fuel must consider SIDs, STARs, weather, routing, and aircraft weight.

sub_modules.title: Fuel Breakdown
sub_modules.content: Fuel is divided into Taxi, Trip, Reserve (Contingency, Alternate, Final Reserve, Additional), and Extra fuel.

sub_modules.title: Taxi Fuel
sub_modules.content: Fuel used before take-off including start-up, taxi, and APU usage.

sub_modules.title: Contingency Fuel
sub_modules.content: Extra fuel to cover unexpected deviations such as weather, routing, or performance variations.

sub_modules.title: Alternate Fuel
sub_modules.content: Fuel required to fly from missed approach at destination to alternate airfield.

sub_modules.title: Final Reserve Fuel
sub_modules.content: Minimum fuel remaining after landing, typically 30 minutes (jets) or 45 minutes (piston) holding.

sub_modules.title: Additional Fuel
sub_modules.content: Extra fuel required in special cases like no alternate or long diversion scenarios.

sub_modules.title: Extra Fuel
sub_modules.content: Any fuel carried beyond minimum regulatory requirements.

sub_modules.title: Contingency Calculation
sub_modules.content: Higher of 5% of trip fuel or 5 minutes holding at 1500 ft.

sub_modules.title: Fuel Monitoring
sub_modules.content: In-flight checks compare actual vs planned fuel consumption and predict fuel at destination.

sub_modules.title: Decision Making
sub_modules.content: If expected fuel is below alternate + final reserve, pilot must consider diversion.

sub_modules.title: FMS Fuel Prediction
sub_modules.content: Modern aircraft systems calculate expected fuel on arrival using real-time data.

sub_modules.title: Howgozit Graph
sub_modules.content: Manual method of tracking fuel consumption using fuel vs distance graph.

sub_modules.title: Decision Point Procedure
sub_modules.content: Allows continuation or diversion decision at a predefined point based on remaining fuel.

sub_modules.title: Isolated Aerodrome Procedure
sub_modules.content: Special fuel planning when no alternate is available, requiring extended reserves.

sub_modules.title: Definition of NAM
sub_modules.content: Nautical Air Miles represent the distance flown through the air based on True Airspeed.

sub_modules.title: Definition of NGM
sub_modules.content: Nautical Ground Miles represent the distance covered over the ground.

sub_modules.title: Still Air Condition
sub_modules.content: When there is no wind, NAM equals NGM.

sub_modules.title: Headwind Effect
sub_modules.content: With headwind, NAM is greater than NGM because aircraft travels more through air but less over ground.

sub_modules.title: Tailwind Effect
sub_modules.content: With tailwind, NAM is less than NGM because aircraft travels less through air but more over ground.

sub_modules.title: NAM-NGM Relationship
sub_modules.content: NAM/NGM = TAS/GS relationship connects air distance and ground distance.

sub_modules.title: Wind Component Impact
sub_modules.content: Wind affects ground distance depending on direction and magnitude.

sub_modules.title: Conversion Formula
sub_modules.content: NGM = NAM ± (Wind Component / 60 × time in minutes).

sub_modules.title: Climb/Descent Cases
sub_modules.content: When TAS or GS is unknown, distance conversion is done using time and wind component.

sub_modules.title: SEP Overview
sub_modules.content: A single-engine piston aircraft with constant speed propeller and adjustable fuel-air mixture.

sub_modules.title: Engine Controls
sub_modules.content: Power is controlled using manifold pressure and RPM, with mixture adjusted for efficiency.

sub_modules.title: Full Throttle Height
sub_modules.content: Altitude at which maximum manifold pressure can no longer be maintained.

sub_modules.title: Aircraft Limits
sub_modules.content: Includes MTOM, MLM, maximum fuel capacity, and fuel density.

sub_modules.title: Climb Performance
sub_modules.content: Graphs provide time, fuel, and distance (NAM) required to climb based on altitude, temperature, and mass.

sub_modules.title: Non-Sea Level Departure
sub_modules.content: Climb data must be adjusted by subtracting airfield values from total climb values.

sub_modules.title: Wind Effect in Climb
sub_modules.content: NAM must be converted to NGM using wind component.

sub_modules.title: Cruise Performance Tables
sub_modules.content: Provide TAS, IAS, and fuel flow based on power setting, altitude, and temperature.

sub_modules.title: Interpolation
sub_modules.content: Required between altitude and temperature values for accuracy.

sub_modules.title: Range Profiles
sub_modules.content: Graphs show maximum still-air range including climb, taxi, and reserve fuel.

sub_modules.title: Wind Adjustment for Range
sub_modules.content: Range in NAM must be converted to NGM using TAS and GS.

sub_modules.title: Endurance
sub_modules.content: Time aircraft can stay airborne including reserve fuel, independent of wind.

sub_modules.title: MEP Overview
sub_modules.content: Twin-engine piston aircraft with constant speed propellers and retractable undercarriage.

sub_modules.title: Aircraft Limits
sub_modules.content: Includes MTOM, MZFM, MLM, fuel capacity, and fuel density.

sub_modules.title: Power Settings
sub_modules.content: 75% High Speed, 65% Economy, 55% Intermediate, 45% Long Range Cruise.

sub_modules.title: Climb Performance
sub_modules.content: Graphs provide fuel, time, and distance for climb based on altitude and temperature.

sub_modules.title: Non-Sea Level Adjustment
sub_modules.content: Climb values must be corrected by subtracting airfield data.

sub_modules.title: Wind Effect
sub_modules.content: Climb and descent distances must be converted from NAM to NGM using wind.

sub_modules.title: Range Graph
sub_modules.content: Provides air range with and without reserves, corrected for ISA deviations.

sub_modules.title: Temperature Correction
sub_modules.content: Add or subtract range based on deviation from ISA.

sub_modules.title: Cruise Fuel Flow
sub_modules.content: Fuel flow depends on power setting and is used to calculate trip fuel.

sub_modules.title: Manifold Pressure Adjustment
sub_modules.content: Adjusted based on temperature deviation from ISA.

sub_modules.title: True Airspeed
sub_modules.content: Extracted from separate graph based on altitude, temperature, and power.

sub_modules.title: Endurance
sub_modules.content: Time aircraft can remain airborne with or without reserve fuel.

sub_modules.title: Descent Performance
sub_modules.content: Fuel, time, and distance calculated similarly to climb using subtraction method.

sub_modules.title: MRJT Overview
sub_modules.content: Modern jet aircraft using FMS and company flight planning systems for optimized routing and fuel management.

sub_modules.title: Aircraft Mass Limits
sub_modules.content: Includes MRM, MTOM, MLM, MZFM, and Dry Operating Mass (DOM).

sub_modules.title: Traffic Load
sub_modules.content: Difference between MZFM and DOM representing passengers, baggage, and cargo.

sub_modules.title: Taxi Fuel
sub_modules.content: Calculated from difference between ramp mass and take-off mass or estimated per minute.

sub_modules.title: Optimum Cruise Altitude
sub_modules.content: Altitude providing best fuel efficiency based on weight and cruise profile.

sub_modules.title: Fuel Penalty
sub_modules.content: Operating above or below optimum altitude increases fuel consumption.

sub_modules.title: Short Distance Cruise
sub_modules.content: Determines maximum achievable cruise altitude for short sectors.

sub_modules.title: Simplified Flight Planning Charts
sub_modules.content: Used to determine trip fuel and time based on distance, altitude, wind, and weight.

sub_modules.title: Wind Correction
sub_modules.content: Adjustments made for headwind or tailwind in fuel and time calculations.

sub_modules.title: Temperature Correction
sub_modules.content: ISA deviations affect performance and must be accounted for.

sub_modules.title: Additional Fuel Adjustments
sub_modules.content: Includes APU usage, taxi fuel, anti-ice, and air conditioning effects.

sub_modules.title: Descent Adjustments
sub_modules.content: Extra fuel required for flaps usage and anti-ice during descent.

sub_modules.title: Holding Fuel
sub_modules.content: Fuel required for holding patterns based on altitude and aircraft weight.

sub_modules.title: Detailed Planning Overview
sub_modules.content: Advanced flight planning using climb tables, cruise tables, descent tables, and wind corrections.

sub_modules.title: En Route Climb Tables
sub_modules.content: Provide time, fuel, distance (NAM), and average TAS based on weight, altitude, and temperature.

sub_modules.title: Climb Adjustments
sub_modules.content: Adjustments required for non-sea level departures and wind components.

sub_modules.title: NAM to NGM Conversion
sub_modules.content: Distance conversion using average TAS and wind component.

sub_modules.title: Integrated Cruise Tables
sub_modules.content: Used to calculate fuel burn based on weight difference and distance.

sub_modules.title: Difference Principle
sub_modules.content: Fuel used equals difference between initial and final aircraft weights.

sub_modules.title: Cruise Methods
sub_modules.content: Includes Long Range Cruise, Mach 0.74, Mach 0.78, and Low-Level cruise.

sub_modules.title: TAS Determination
sub_modules.content: Extracted from tables and corrected for temperature deviations.

sub_modules.title: Wind Corrections
sub_modules.content: Ground distance must be converted to air distance before using tables.

sub_modules.title: Temperature Corrections
sub_modules.content: Adjustments applied to TAS and fuel flow for ISA deviations.

sub_modules.title: Descent Tables
sub_modules.content: Provide fuel, time, and distance for descent phase.

sub_modules.title: Step-by-Step Method
sub_modules.content: Systematic approach to solving MRJT detailed planning problems.

sub_modules.title: ETOPS Introduction
sub_modules.content: Rules governing twin-engine aircraft operations over remote areas beyond one-hour diversion distance.

sub_modules.title: ETOPS Definitions
sub_modules.content: Key terms such as threshold distance, rule time, adequate and suitable airfields.

sub_modules.title: ETOPS Segment
sub_modules.content: Portion of flight beyond threshold distance from adequate aerodrome.

sub_modules.title: ETOPS Fuel Policy
sub_modules.content: Fuel planning including critical fuel, contingency, alternate, and reserve fuel.

sub_modules.title: Critical Point (CP/ETP)
sub_modules.content: Most critical point where diversion time to alternates is equal.

sub_modules.title: Critical Fuel Calculation
sub_modules.content: Fuel required from CP to diversion considering worst-case scenarios.

sub_modules.title: Diversion Distance
sub_modules.content: Maximum distance allowed from an alternate based on time and speed.

sub_modules.title: In-flight Diversion
sub_modules.content: Fuel and time calculations during diversion with one engine inoperative.

sub_modules.title: Non-normal Operations
sub_modules.content: Special scenarios like gear-down ferry flights and their performance impact.

sub_modules.title: Fuel Tankering
sub_modules.content: Economic analysis of carrying extra fuel based on cost differences.

sub_modules.title: Computer Flight Planning
sub_modules.content: Automated flight planning systems and required inputs.

sub_modules.title: Flight Plan Interpretation
sub_modules.content: Understanding detailed computer-generated flight plans.

sub_modules.title: Flight Plan Validation
sub_modules.content: Checking accuracy of computer-generated flight plans.

sub_modules.title: Chart Introduction
sub_modules.content: Understanding Jeppesen VFR charts, projection type, scale, and operational limits.

sub_modules.title: WGS84 Coordinates
sub_modules.content: Global coordinate system used for navigation points, aerodromes, and navaids.

sub_modules.title: Track and Distance Measurement
sub_modules.content: Methods to determine true and magnetic track and measure distance.

sub_modules.title: Magnetic Variation
sub_modules.content: Conversion between true and magnetic direction using isogonic lines.

sub_modules.title: Aeronautical Information
sub_modules.content: Interpretation of airports, waypoints, navaids, and airspace types.

sub_modules.title: Airspace Types
sub_modules.content: Restricted, danger, and prohibited areas and their implications.

sub_modules.title: Terrain and Elevation
sub_modules.content: Understanding terrain contours, spot elevations, and obstacle clearance.

sub_modules.title: Grid MORA
sub_modules.content: Minimum grid area altitude ensuring terrain clearance.

sub_modules.title: Minimum Flight Altitude
sub_modules.content: Factors affecting safe altitude selection including terrain and navigation accuracy.

sub_modules.title: Cruising Levels
sub_modules.content: Semi-circular rule for selecting flight levels based on track direction.

sub_modules.title: Altimeter Errors
sub_modules.content: Errors caused by pressure and temperature deviations from ISA.

sub_modules.title: Pressure Error
sub_modules.content: Altitude errors due to QNH differences from standard pressure.

sub_modules.title: Temperature Error
sub_modules.content: Altitude errors due to non-standard temperature conditions.

sub_modules.title: Altitude Corrections
sub_modules.content: Use of CRP5 or calculations to determine true altitude.

sub_modules.title: Airways Introduction
sub_modules.content: Understanding structured routes used for IFR navigation using Jeppesen charts.

sub_modules.title: Jeppesen Chart Glossary
sub_modules.content: Key ICAO definitions and abbreviations used in aviation charts.

sub_modules.title: Altitudes and Flight Levels
sub_modules.content: Definitions of altitude, height, elevation, and flight levels.

sub_modules.title: Pressure Settings
sub_modules.content: Understanding QNH, QFE, and QNE in flight operations.

sub_modules.title: Navaid Symbols
sub_modules.content: Identification of VOR, NDB, DME, ILS, and other navigation aids.

sub_modules.title: Communication Information
sub_modules.content: Understanding frequencies and communication via navaids.

sub_modules.title: Airways and Routes
sub_modules.content: Structure of airways, reporting points, and route designators.

sub_modules.title: Airway Altitudes
sub_modules.content: MEA, MOCA, MORA, and MAA definitions and usage.

sub_modules.title: Airspace Classification
sub_modules.content: Classes A to G and their operational differences.

sub_modules.title: Controlled Airspace
sub_modules.content: CTR, CTA, TMA and their significance.

sub_modules.title: ATS Routes
sub_modules.content: Lower and Upper ATS routes and their characteristics.

sub_modules.title: Conditional and Advisory Routes
sub_modules.content: Understanding CDR and advisory routes in flight planning.

sub_modules.title: SID and STAR Charts
sub_modules.content: Standard procedures for departure and arrival.

sub_modules.title: Approach Charts
sub_modules.content: Understanding approach procedures and landing minima.

sub_modules.title: Polar High Altitude Charts
sub_modules.content: Charts used for polar navigation with grid navigation techniques.

sub_modules.title: Plotting Charts
sub_modules.content: Charts used to plot routes, positions, and navigation data.

sub_modules.title: Lambert Conformal Projection
sub_modules.content: Projection where great circles are straight lines and scale is nearly constant.

sub_modules.title: North Atlantic Plotting Charts
sub_modules.content: Charts used for ETOPS and ETP/CP calculations.

sub_modules.title: Equal Time Point (ETP)
sub_modules.content: Point where time to two alternates is equal.

sub_modules.title: Critical Point (CP)
sub_modules.content: Point where fuel/time constraints are most critical.

sub_modules.title: Range and Time Circles
sub_modules.content: Circles representing diversion capability based on time and distance.

sub_modules.title: Atlantic Orientation Charts
sub_modules.content: Charts used for transatlantic route planning.

sub_modules.title: NAT Organized Track System
sub_modules.content: Structured tracks for transatlantic flights.

sub_modules.title: Communication and Procedures
sub_modules.content: Standard communication, reporting, and contingency procedures.

sub_modules.title: ATC Flight Plan Introduction
sub_modules.content: A standardized format used to inform ATC of flight intentions.

sub_modules.title: Types of Flight Plans
sub_modules.content: Individual flight plans and repetitive flight plans (RPL).

sub_modules.title: Flight Plan Definitions
sub_modules.content: Key terms like EET, EOBT, ETA, and current flight plan.

sub_modules.title: ICAO Flight Plan Format
sub_modules.content: Structure and fields required in ICAO flight plans.

sub_modules.title: Aircraft Identification
sub_modules.content: Rules for entering aircraft ID and call signs.

sub_modules.title: Route (Item 15)
sub_modules.content: Detailed route including airways, points, and changes.

sub_modules.title: Use of DCT
sub_modules.content: Rules for direct routing between points.

sub_modules.title: Speed and Level Changes
sub_modules.content: How to indicate changes in speed and altitude.

sub_modules.title: Supplementary Information
sub_modules.content: Emergency and survival details (Item 19).

sub_modules.title: Flight Plan Decoding
sub_modules.content: Understanding a filled ATC flight plan.

chapter_name: Chapter 12 - ATC Flight Plan Annex (ICAO Rules)

sub_modules.title: Flight Plan Submission Rules
sub_modules.content: Procedures for submitting flight plans before and during flight.

sub_modules.title: Flight Plan Acceptance
sub_modules.content: How ATS validates and accepts flight plans.

sub_modules.title: RNP Requirements
sub_modules.content: Navigation accuracy standards for specific airspace.

sub_modules.title: Repetitive Flight Plans (RPL)
sub_modules.content: Rules for regularly scheduled IFR flights.

sub_modules.title: Changes to Flight Plans
sub_modules.content: Handling delays, cancellations, and modifications.

sub_modules.title: Item 7 to 19 Detailed Instructions
sub_modules.content: Exact ICAO format for completing flight plan fields.

sub_modules.title: Equipment Codes
sub_modules.content: Standard and advanced navigation and communication equipment codes.

sub_modules.title: Route Encoding Rules
sub_modules.content: How to encode routes using ATS routes, DCT, and coordinates.

sub_modules.title: Supplementary Information
sub_modules.content: Emergency, survival, and aircraft information.

sub_modules.title: RPL Listing Instructions
sub_modules.content: How to manage repetitive flight plan submissions.

sub_modules.title: Definition of PET
sub_modules.content: Point where time to continue or return is equal.

sub_modules.title: Purpose of PET
sub_modules.content: Used for decision-making during emergencies.

sub_modules.title: Effect of Wind on PET
sub_modules.content: PET shifts depending on wind direction and strength.

sub_modules.title: All-Engine PET Calculation
sub_modules.content: PET using normal cruise performance.

sub_modules.title: Engine Failure PET
sub_modules.content: PET using reduced performance after engine failure.

sub_modules.title: PET Formula
sub_modules.content: Core equation used to calculate PET distance.

sub_modules.title: Definition of PSR
sub_modules.content: Furthest point aircraft can fly and return within safe fuel limits.

sub_modules.title: Safe Endurance vs Total Endurance
sub_modules.content: Safe endurance excludes reserve fuel.

sub_modules.title: Purpose of PSR
sub_modules.content: Used when destination may become unavailable.

sub_modules.title: PSR Formula
sub_modules.content: Core formula to calculate PSR distance.

sub_modules.title: Effect of Wind on PSR
sub_modules.content: Wind reduces PSR distance regardless of direction.

sub_modules.title: PSR Calculation Steps
sub_modules.content: Step-by-step method to solve PSR problems.
